data.title: Torrid misses Q3 expectations, issues 'prudent' Q4 and FY24 guidance
data.description:
A lack of “newness” and a “meaningful” change in the environment influenced Q3 results for Torrid Holdings (NYSE:CURV), dragging shares down by nearly 20% in Tuesday’s after-hours trading.

“Our third quarter results were below our expectations as our fall assortments did not offer enough newness and novelty,” said CEO Lisa Harper, adding that the volatility in the business coupled with a considerable amount of the quarter left, led the company to take a prudent approach to its Q4 outlook.

The company expects sales to be between $255M and $270M, falling short of the $284.3M consensus estimate. Adjusted EBITDA is forecasted to be between $9M and $15M.

For the full year, sales are expected to range between $1.083B and $1.098B, versus the consensus estimate of $1.13B. Adjusted EBITDA is targeted for $101M and $107M. The company also plans to open 12 to 16 new stores while closing 30 to 40, balancing the placement of outdoor centers and enclosed mall locations.

For Q3, Torrid (CURV) loss per share narrowed to a penny from a loss of 3 cents per share, but missed expectations for a profit of 3 cents per share. Sales were down 4.2% to $263.8M versus $284.3M estimates as comparable sales dropped 6.5% in the fiscal third quarter. The increase in sales for regular-priced merchandise lifted the company’s gross profit margin by 285 basis points to 36.1%. Adjusted EBITDA increased to $19.6M from $19.4M a year ago.

data.title: Box Q3 results surpass expectations, reaches record operating margin
data.description:
Box (NYSE:BOX) shares slid during early post-market trading Tuesday after the company reported third quarter fiscal 2025 financial results that surpassed expectations.

For the quarter ended October 31, Box reported adjusted earnings per share of $0.45 which was more than the consensus estimate of $0.42. Revenue of $275.9M was also more than the estimate of $275.1M.

For the quarter in progress, Box expects earnings per share of $0.41, which is less than the estimate of $0.42. The company expects revenue of $279M, which is also just below the estimate of $279.1M.

However, the company's full-year earnings per share guidance was stronger than the consensus estimate. It expects adjusted earnings per share of $1.70 versus the consensus of $1.67. Its full-year revenue projection of $1.09B was in line with the estimate.

"With operational discipline built into the core of our company, we drove record gross and operating margins in the quarter," said Box CFO Dylan Smith. "Our efficient cost structure enables us to continue to make meaningful investments in our sales and marketing programs and product roadmap as we deliver the leading Intelligent Content Cloud for the enterprise."

Box indicated about one-third of its revenue is generated outside the U.S. causing the company to experience some foreign currency exchange rate headwinds.

data.title: Cabot Corp increases share repurchase authorization by 10M shares
data.description:
• Cabot Corporation (NYSE:CBT) said on Tuesday that its board has authorized the company to repurchase up to 10M additional shares of the company’s common stock, increasing the current amount of shares available for repurchase to approximately 11M.
  • Shares will be repurchased in the open market or in privately negotiated transactions using the company’s existing sources of liquidity and free cash flow generated in the future.

data.title: Stewart extends contract for CEO Fred Eppinger
data.description:
• Stewart Information Services Corporation (NYSE:STC) today announced its Board of Directors has agreed with Frederick H. Eppinger, Chief Executive Officer, to amend and restate his employment agreement, extending the term for another three years through the end of 2028.
  • Eppinger took over as CEO in September of 2019 after having served as a director of Stewart since 2016.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CRM in charts: Subscription and support revenue climbs 9.1% Y/Y
UnitedHealth issues 2025 guidance ahead of investor meeting
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) has issued its 2025 outlook ahead of the company’s annual investor conference on Dec. 4.

The health insurer said it sees reporting 2025 revenue of $450 billion to $455 billion, with GAAP EPS of $28.15 to $28.65 and non-GAAP EPS of $29.50 to $30.00, according to a statement.

Analysts, on average, were expecting UnitedHealth to report 2025 revenue of $430.41B and non-GAAP EPS of $29.95.

The company still expects 2024 GAAP EPS of $15.50 to $15.75 per share and non-GAAP EPS $27.50 to $27.75 per share.

Analysts, on average, are expecting 2024 non-GAAP EPS of $27.61 and revenue of $339.4M.

data.title: Prudential Financial names Andrew Sullivan its next CEO)
data.description:
Prudential Financial's (NYSE:PRU) board appointed Andrew Sullivan its next CEO, effective March 31, 2025, succeeding Charles F. Lowrey, the company said Tuesday.

Lowrey will remain as executive chairman of the board for 18 months.

Sullivan currently serves as executive vice president and head of International Businesses and Global Investment Management. In his current role, he oversees the company’s International Insurance segment and PGIM, the firm’s $1.4T global investment management business. Since joining the company in 2011, he has held successive leadership positions, including heading all of the firm’s U.S.-based businesses.

Caroline Feeney will expand her role to become global head of Insurance and Retirement, a newly created position overseeing Prudential's (NYSE:PRU) domestic and international insurance and retirement business, effective March 31, 2025. She and incoming head of PGIM Jacques Chappuis will report to Sullivan in his role as CEO.

Last month, the company announced that Chappuis, a former Morgan Stanley executive, would become PGIM's president and CEO.

Vice Chair Robert Falzon will step down from the board, effective March 31, 2025, and retire from Prudential (PRU), effective July 11, 2025.

data.title: Intuitive Machines slips on launch of public offering and concurrent private placement
data.description:
• Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ:LUNR) has commenced an underwritten public offering of $65M of shares of its class A common stock.
  • The company and a selling stockholder intend to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional $8.88M and $877,500 of shares of Class A Common Stock from the company and such selling stockholder, respectively.
  • On December 2, 2024, the company also entered into an agreement with Boryung Corporation, together with its affiliates, to sell to Boryung $10M of shares of class A common stock in a concurrent private placement at a purchase price per share equal to the public offering price per share in the offering.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds it receives from the offering and the private placement, together with its existing cash, cash equivalents and short-term investment balance, to acquire an equivalent number of newly-issued common units of Intuitive Machines, LLC from Intuitive Machines OpCo.
  • LUNR shares fell 14.8% in after hours trading on Tuesday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Genpact signs collaboration agreement with AWS to accelerate AI adoption
data.description:
• Genpact (NYSE:G) announced a multi-year collaboration agreement with Amazon Web Services (AWS) with plans to accelerate the adoption of AI.
  • By simplifying access to cutting-edge data and AI technologies, Genpact and AWS are allowing a broad range of decision-makers to drive innovation within their organizations.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Pure Storage surges as Q3 results, guidance top Wall Street estimates
data.description:
Pure Storage (NYSE:PSTG) surged more than 23% in late trading on Tuesday after the data storage company reported fiscal third-quarter results and guidance that topped Wall Street's expectations.

For the period ending November 3, Pure Storage said it earned an adjusted $0.50 per share as revenue rose 8.9% year-over-year to come in at $831.1M. While product revenue ticked up just 0.3% year-over-year to come in at $454.7M,subscription services revenue jumped 22% year-over-year to $376.3M during the period.

Subscription annual recurring revenue was $1.6B, while remaining performance obligations rose 16% year-over-year to $2.4B.

Analysts were expecting earnings of $0.42 per share on $814.8M in revenue.

"Pure Storage has achieved another industry first in our journey of data storage innovation with a transformational design win for our DirectFlash technology in a top-four hyperscaler," said Pure Storage Chairman and CEO Charles Giancarlo in a statement. "This win is the vanguard for Pure Flash technology to become the standard for all hyperscaler online storage, providing unparalleled performance and scalability while also reducing operating costs and power consumption."

Looking to the fourth-quarter, Pure Storage expects to generate $867M in revenue, up 9.7% year-over-year and above the $856.9M estimate. Full-year revenue is forecast to rise 11.5% year-over-year to $3.15B, above the $3.13B estimate.

Pure Storage also said it expects an adjusted operating margin of 15.6% and 17% for the fourth-quarter and full-year, respectively.

The company will hold a conference call at 5 p.m. EST to discuss the results.
